ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "test_case_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the test case */,
  "link_to_spec" INTEGER /* Link to the specification document */,
  "xforms_basic" INTEGER /* Whether the test case is normative for XForms Basic */,
  "xforms_full" INTEGER /* Whether the test case is normative for XForms Full */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the test case (e.g., functional, performance) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test case */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the test case */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was last modified */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., active, deprecated, under review) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_link_to_spec FOREIGN KEY ("link_to_spec") REFERENCES specifications ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification */,
  "spec_name" TEXT /* Name of the specification */,
  "spec_format" TEXT /* Format of the specification (e.g., PDF, HTML) */,
  "spec_file" TEXT /* File path to the specification document */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the specification document */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the specification */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the specification was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the specification */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the specification was last modified */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the specification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case that was executed */,
  "result_file" TEXT /* File path to the result file (e.g., screenshot, log) */,
  "result_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was executed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the test result (e.g., pass, fail, inconclusive) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments on the test result */,
  "executed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who executed the test */,
  "test_environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was executed (e.g., development, staging, production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_executed_by FOREIGN KEY ("executed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test case */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test case was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, execute) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used for access */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_test_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
